narration_text: Now we'll see how the transport-layer protocol works. Look at the diagram on your slide — notice how the transport-layer segment is created by combining the message M with the network-layer header Hn. This is essentially how data is packaged for transmission across the network. I want you to focus on the different layers involved in this process, from the application layer down to the link layer. Notice how each layer adds its own header to the data, allowing it to be routed and delivered correctly. Let's think about this together, considering how these layers work in tandem to ensure reliable data transfer. As we move forward, we'll explore how this process enables efficient communication over the internet.
